rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical 1,2-dichlorocyclization/dichlorination of enyne substrates using catalytic aryl iodide (p-Tol-I) as a redox mediator generating an aryl-λ3-iodane (ArICl2) that delivers chlorine radicals/atoms under undivided-cell electrolysis with Et3N·HCl as Cl source.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of aryl iodide (p-Tol-I) to a hypervalent dichloro(aryl)-λ3-iodane (ArICl2) using chloride from Et3N·HCl; this hypervalent iodine mediator then transfers Cl to the organic substrate. Cathode reduces protons/Et3NH+ to H2.

key_intermediates.name: vinyl/alkenyl chloride radical adduct from Cl• addition to alkyne
carbon-centered radical after 5-exo-trig cyclization onto pendant alkene

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes p-Tol-I in the presence of Cl- (from Et3N·HCl) to generate ArICl2; this λ3-iodane homolytically delivers a Cl• to the alkyne of the enyne, producing a vinyl radical that cyclizes 5-exo onto the alkene; the resulting C-radical abstracts/captures a second Cl from ArICl2, regenerating p-Tol-I.
rate_determining_step: Chlorine atom transfer from ArICl2 to the alkyne (Cl• addition generating vinyl radical)

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction of 1a fully suppressed (2a N.R.); HRMS detected 1-chloro-2,2-diphenylethylene adduct (calc 215.0622, found 215.0626), trapping a Cl• / vinyl-Cl species.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction suppressed (N.R.) under standard electrolysis; with stoichiometric ArICl2 + BHT product still forms (45%), consistent with mediator-derived radical pathway.

probe_reagents.name: Stoichiometric 4-Me-C6H4ICl2 (preformed ArICl2)
probe_reagents.observation: Without current but with 2 equiv ArICl2 the reaction proceeds in 52% yield (entry 5), supporting ArICl2 as the active chlorinating intermediate; isolated in 20% yield, 49% ee in a separate stoichiometric run.

probe_reagents.name: Selectfluor / CsCl (alternative oxidant)
probe_reagents.observation: Without current, replacing Et3N·HCl with Selectfluor + CsCl gives 23% yield, indicating chemical oxidation of ArI to ArICl2 reproduces reactivity.

probe_reagents.name: No p-Tol-I control
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction still proceeds in 21% yield without mediator, indicating a minor direct/background pathway, but mediator strongly accelerates the reaction.

probe_reagents.name: No Et3N·HCl control
probe_reagents.observation: No detectable product (N.D.), confirming chloride is the Cl source.

probe_reagents.name: No current control
probe_reagents.observation: No reaction without electrolysis, confirming requirement of anodic oxidation to generate ArICl2.

notes: In-situ formation of ArICl2 was directly observed by 1H NMR of the crude electrolysis mixture (p-Tol-I + Et3N·HCl in DCM/HFIP, 10 mA, 2 h). Control experiments collectively support a mediator-based Cl-atom-transfer (radical) mechanism rather than direct anodic oxidation of substrate: (i) radical traps (DPE, BHT) shut down reaction and trap a Cl-containing radical; (ii) preformed ArICl2 substitutes for current; (iii) alternative chemical oxidants (Selectfluor/CsCl) recover reactivity. An alternative ionic pathway via ArICl2-mediated chloriranium ion cannot be fully excluded but is disfavored by radical-trapping outcomes and modest ee (49%).
